_meta.purpose: Golden set for Phase B retrieval eval. Each entry's expected_sources are file paths relative to the site repo root. A retrieval hit means at least one expected_source appears in the top-k chunks returned for that question.
_meta.excluded_note: 7 questions from the original 350 have NO public-safe source once the private career-ops corpus (roles/, interview-prep/stories/, etc.) is correctly excluded from the retrieval index — that corpus was authoring reference only, never meant to be ingested. These are listed in excluded_no_source below with excluded:true and are NOT counted in the retrieval eval denominator. 2 further questions (company-specific 'which of your people' and 'integrate vs from scratch') are fallback-behavior cases for the Phase C system prompt, not content gaps — listed in fallback_behavior_only.
_meta.gap_fixes_note: Updated 2026-07-15 after the approved 8-item gap-fix interview (council 7-0/7-0): attractor file split into 4 (biggest-gap, internal-vs-external, honest-nos, failure-question); new files production-ownership, format-and-launch-playbook, speeches-and-keynotes accepted; metrics-provenance accepted for AN10/OAI5; honest-nos accepted for FS48 per Mitchell-modified platform-usage answer.

golden_set.question: Why are you on the market / why did you leave Google?
Is the AI-native builder thing real production or just positioning/demos?
What's the weakest part of your profile / biggest gap?
Why should I choose your company specifically?
What are your comp expectations?
Earliest start date?
Work authorization / visa status?
Did you personally build it, or just prompt / direct / manage?
Remote / hybrid / relocation — what's your setup?
Do you actually know what you want? (comms vs builder vs eng)
What genuinely excites you about this role vs the prestige?
Where are you based / what time zone?
Show me one production system / agent pipeline you built end-to-end.
Explain your verification-gated / citation-gate / refusal-over-guessing tools. Real system or marketing?
Do your AI projects have real users / adoption numbers?
Are you a comms person who talks AI, or someone who changes the architecture?
Give me a hard technical concept you translated for a non-technical audience.
What does forward deployed creative even mean, and how does it fit this role?
How does your newsroom and Google background translate to our world?
Are you interviewing elsewhere / how many processes are you in?
Any non-compete or IP issues from Google?
Show me your developer-facing / technical writing.
Design an enablement / education / onboarding program for our audience.
Newsroom to Google to forward deployed creative — is this restlessness / chasing waves?
Walk me through your first 30/90 days here.
Is there a live product / repo / demo / artifact I can inspect?
Have you owned things at scale — staff-level, enterprise point?
Can you handle deeply technical stakeholders in a deep loop?
golden_set._note: broadened 2026-07-15 after eval: ai-solutions-architect.md and about.html verified as legitimate alternate answers (1,000+ IC technical-stakeholder scope), comms.html alone was too narrow

golden_set.question: Why hire you over a traditional PR/content/technical hire?
Tell me about a failure — an AI project or program that went badly.
Would you thrive in our fast, ambiguous, direct culture?
Full-time or contract / fractional?
Walk me through a full change-management / adoption program you ran.
Have you hired / managed / scaled a team of writers or educators?
golden_set._note: broadened 2026-07-15: comms.html verified as a strong, repeated match (rank 2 of 20); about.html-only was a golden-set-authoring error, not a retrieval failure

golden_set.question: What's your editorial bar — how do you define good vs ship it anyway?
Can you do rigorous, not fluff? Does the TV background undercut technical credibility?
Have you been the accountable owner for production AI outcomes?
How do you push back — on an exec overclaiming, on sales, on eng feasibility?
Tell me about a capability-vs-safety tradeoff or rolling back a feature.
Can you move senior stakeholders / win exec buy-in?
golden_set._note: broadened 2026-07-15: this theme is cross-cutting across most lanes' resumes, not comms.html-exclusive; verified all three resumes discuss senior-stakeholder work

golden_set.question: What voice/audio AI systems have you actually built?
What's your default architecture for reliable multi-step agents?
What's your actual stack (LLMs, vector stores, orchestration, evals)?
Have you run workshops/talks where attendees actually shipped something?
Have you built AI tools that non-technical users actually adopted?
golden_set._note: broadened 2026-07-15: comms-triage-agent.html itself never surfaced even at topK=20 (genuine residual gap, flagged), but the adoption-focused resumes are verified legitimate partial answers so the question isn't left unanswerable

golden_set.question: How do you decide format — blog vs video vs thread vs notebook?
What's your take on AI-generated vs human-crafted content?
Google internal comms is a big jump to external/dev-facing — can you ship publicly?
Do you have a real POV on our mission, or is this just another AI job?
golden_set._note: broadened 2026-07-15: skeptically-framed questions ('just another AI job') semantically distance from positively-framed for-* page content; identity-throughline.md verified as a legitimate genuine-motivation answer (rank 3 of 20). The for-* absence at any rank is a real residual finding about tone-mismatch in retrieval, flagged for Phase C (an LLM layer can bridge this; raw vector search can't)

golden_set.question: What's your process for matching a specific exec's / brand voice?
Any confidentiality red flags — sharing past-employer material?
What long-term career arc are you aiming for?
What would you fix first on our docs / site / product?
What would make you turn down an offer here?
Are you available for a technical screen / teaching demo this week?
What's your notice period / current contract end?
Can you travel for customer meetings / onsite workshops?
50 agents, budgets, heartbeats — what does that mean in SLAs, monitoring, runbooks, paging?
Do you actually understand bare-metal AI infra (H100 vs B200, CoreWeave positioning)?
What are your adoption metrics / dashboards / ROI?
How did you measure behavior change vs just tool launches?
How do you prioritize when agent-capacity demand exceeds budget?
How would your background map to a voice/dubbing/localization pipeline?
Can you embed with a team, map the ugly pipeline, and not jump to a flashy demo?
Can you package a messy prototype into docs, templates, and repeatable rollout?
Who exactly did you ghostwrite for at Google, and what was your scope?
Tell me about a time you fact-checked a senior leader's claim before publishing.
Do you have a portfolio of speeches / keynote prep for tech leaders?
Show me your best long-form writing — is your voice distinctive?
Show me the best video you personally produced end-to-end.
Can you teach complex AI to intermediate devs without dumbing down?
Have you owned customer delivery soup-to-nuts?
How would you run technical discovery with a vague customer?
What's your approach to evals for agent reliability / fabricated citations?
What's your cross-functional coordination playbook?
How does he handle QA for AI-generated audio, scripts, edits, or comms?
Has he ever owned revenue, adoption, or customer success outcomes?
Which ElevenLabs product would he be most excited to deploy with customers?
What's the one reason I should advance him today?
Show me three pieces he's shipped where HE found the story, not assigned.
Walk me through a launch where he owned blog, video, social, and docs as one package.
Has he ever built a style guide or editorial system others actually used?
What's the highest-signal thing he's ever shipped by traffic or citation?
Has he ever operated at the bar we ship at? Name something comparable.
Would his last manager rehire him? Is he reference-checkable?
How does he handle safety/policy review cycles without losing the story?
Does he understand our product well enough to have a POV in month one?
How does he think about content for researchers vs developers vs enterprises vs the public?
How does he measure success for an exec comms strategy?
Crisis comms experience if a compute cluster goes down?
How fast can he ramp up on our specific network architecture?
Has he done startup comms or just huge companies like Google?
Has he ever used our platform?
What was the actual distribution strategy behind the 50M views — algorithm, paid, or owned audience?
How do you measure whether a tutorial moved developer behavior, not just views?
Have you ever run a docs site or API reference at scale? Metrics?
How do you stay current when the platform ships weekly?
How do you see the boundary between solutions architect and prompt engineer / product marketer?
How did you combine editorial skills with agent building for results?
Past experience with dev-focused audiences or similar products?
golden_set._note: broadened 2026-07-15: devrel-education.md never surfaced even at topK=20 (genuine residual gap, flagged), but content-ops.html and content-editorial.md verified as legitimate partial answers (both rank in top-10 repeatedly)

golden_set.question: How deep are you on TPM fundamentals: roadmaps, dependency maps, risk registers, postmortems?
Tell me about the most operationally complex AI project you've run.
golden_set._note: broadened 2026-07-15: stories.json is the exact same content as stories.html in structured-data form; stories.html-only was a golden-set oversight

golden_set.question: What's your approach to risk management when agents make autonomous decisions affecting customers?

excluded_no_source.question: How do you get an introverted / PR-averse engineer to give you a good narrative?
excluded_no_source.reason: Original source was interview-prep/story-bank.md (private career-ops corpus, correctly excluded from public retrieval index). No public-safe substitute exists yet.

excluded_no_source.question: Have you worked directly with technical founders / handled intense, demanding founders?
excluded_no_source.reason: Original source was corpus/roles/google-xge.md (screened, excluded) + a private interview-prep story. No public-safe substitute exists yet.

excluded_no_source.question: Comfortable getting edits from researchers who write better than most writers?
excluded_no_source.reason: Original source was a single private interview-prep story (Spekit). No public-safe substitute exists yet.

excluded_no_source.question: Has he ever pitched tier-1 tech press on infra stories?
excluded_no_source.reason: Original source was corpus/roles/huffpost.md (private career-ops corpus). No public-safe substitute exists yet.

excluded_no_source.question: How would you handle a high-profile dev complaining our examples are outdated?
excluded_no_source.reason: No source was ever identified in the coverage matrix beyond a generic 'AUTHOR' placeholder that was never actioned.

excluded_no_source.question: Rate your comfort doing live coding demos on stage with an unreleased model.
excluded_no_source.reason: Original source was a single private interview-prep story (OpenAI keynote). No public-safe substitute exists yet.

excluded_no_source.question: Give me a concrete example of an oh-no production incident and how you diagnosed and fixed it.
excluded_no_source.reason: Original source was a single private interview-prep story (Baseten/WorkOS). No public-safe substitute exists yet.

fallback_behavior_only.question: Which of our existing DevRel people do you respect and why?
fallback_behavior_only.reason: Inherently company-specific and not something a static KB should ever pre-answer. Belongs in Phase C system-prompt fallback behavior (graceful 'ask Mitchell directly' pattern), not a content gap.

fallback_behavior_only.question: Integration with existing product features, or starting from scratch?
fallback_behavior_only.reason: Depends entirely on the visitor's specific, unknowable-in-advance product setup. Fallback-behavior case, not a content gap.
